draft.introduction: A hymn celebrating God’s mercy and compassion. The phrase “as a father shows compassion” offers one of the Old Testament’s most tender images of the divine-human relationship.

translation.text: Bless the Lord, O my soul, and all that is within me, bless his holy name! Bless the Lord, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits, who forgives all your iniquity, who heals all your diseases, who redeems your life from the pit.
translation.original_text: εὐλόγει ἡ ψυχή μου τὸν κύριον

translation.text: As a father shows compassion to his children, so the Lord shows compassion to those who fear him. For he knows our frame; he remembers that we are dust. As for man, his days are like grass; but the steadfast love of the Lord is from everlasting to everlasting.
translation.original_text: καθὼς οἰκτίρει πατὴρ υἱούς ὠκτίρησεν κύριος

translator_notes.note: “He remembers that we are dust” (χοῦς) echoes Genesis 2:7 and 3:19. The Fathers read this as God’s compassion arising from knowledge of human frailty—the same frailty the Incarnation was meant to heal.
